name: Agent4Edu Model and Workflow Effects Extract
population_or_context: Learner response generation in personalized learning and computerized adaptive testing contexts.
sample_size: The existing source record reports EduData with 18,045 response records from 500 Chinese high-school students; the model-version comparison in arXiv HTML Table 1 is marked as a 100-learner exploratory condition for the GPT-4 row.
methods: Extracted the model-version and agent-workflow evidence from Agent4Edu, focusing on GPT-3.5-turbo and GPT-4 conditions plus profile, memory, learning-status reflection, and action-module prompting.
education_context: High-school online learning platform response records and computerized adaptive testing service evaluation.
response_origin: LLM-powered generative agents simulate learner responses and are compared with real learner response records.
rubric_label_space: Exercise response correctness and knowledge prediction outcomes rather than open-ended rubric scoring.
label_source: Reference exercise answers, learner response records, and cognitive-diagnosis tools provide comparison signals.
generation_model: GPT-3.5-turbo is the main reported Agent4Edu condition; GPT-4 appears in a 100-learner exploratory comparison.
generation_method: Generative-agent workflow with learner profile, factual memory, short-term memory, long-term memory, learning-status reflection, knowledge-proficiency tools, and action prompts.
prompt_strategy: Profile-, memory-, proficiency-, and action-conditioned prompts with corrective reflection when a generated action requires correction.
evaluation_method: Prediction scores are reported for learner simulation, and downstream CAT models are retrained with generated learner data to test service improvement.
scorer_use: Generated data are used for learner simulation and CAT/personalized learning algorithm augmentation, not automated scorer validation.
real_response_comparator: Real EduData learner records provide the comparator for simulated response behavior.
reported_metrics: The arXiv HTML reports Agent4Edu (GPT-3.5-turbo) at 73.88 ACC, Agent4Edu (GPT-3.5-turbo)100 at 74.57 ACC, and Agent4Edu (GPT-4)100 at 82.43 ACC in Table 1; Table 4 reports CAT F1-score improvements after augmentation.
limitations: The GPT-4 result is explicitly tied to a 100-learner exploratory condition and should not be generalized as a full-scale model ranking.
The comparison changes model version and sample condition together, so the staged finding treats it as model-version sensitivity rather than a clean controlled model benchmark.
The evidence is learner-response and CAT specific, not open-response scoring or essay-generation evidence.
